title: Composite elastomer and membranes and membrane systems containing a composite elastomer
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a composite elastomer including a plurality of polymers covalently bound to a plurality of platelet-like fillers via ethanolamine bonds, ethanolamide bonds, or a combination thereof, to form the crosslinked composite elastomer. The present disclosure further provides a method of forming a composite elastomer. The method includes reacting amine or amide functional groups, or a combination thereof, on a plurality of polymers with epoxide functional groups on a plurality of platelet-like fillers to form an ethanolamine or ethanolamide bond and thereby crosslink the plurality of polymers via the plurality of platelet-like fillers. The disclosure further provides a gas separation membrane including the composite elastomer and a gas separation system including such a gas separation membrane. The disclosure further provides a gas barrier membrane including the composite elastomer.
claims: Claims 1. A composite elastomer comprising a plurality of polymers covalently bound to a plurality of platelet-like fillers via ethanolamine bonds, ethanolamide bonds, or a combination thereof, to form a crosslinked composite elastomer. 2. The composite elastomer of Claim 1, wherein the plurality of polymers comprises a single type of polymer. 3. The composite elastomer of Claim 1, wherein the plurality of polymers comprises at least two types of polymers, all types of which are covalently bound to the plurality of platelet-like fillers via ethanolamine bonds, ethanolamide bonds, or a combination thereof. 4. The composite elastomer of Claim 1, wherein the plurality of polymers comprises at least two types of polymers, a first type which is covalently bound to the plurality of platelet like fillers via ethanolamine bonds, ethanolamide bonds, or a combination thereof, and a second type which is covalently bound to the first type of polymer, but not to the plurality of platelet like fillers. 5. The composite elastomer of Claim 1, wherein the plurality of polymers are covalently bound to the plurality of platelet-like fillers via ethanolamine bonds, ethanolamide bonds, or a combination thereof located on at least one end of the plurality of polymers. 6. The composite elastomer of Claim 1, wherein the plurality of polymers are covalently bound to the plurality of platelet-like fillers via ethanolamine bonds, ethanolamide bonds, or a combination thereof located at both ends of the plurality of polymers. 7. The composite elastomer of Claim 1, wherein the same type of ethanolamine bonds or ethanolamide bonds are at both ends of the plurality of polymers. 8. The composite elastomer of Claim 1, wherein the plurality of polymers include at least one of the following types of polymers: polydimethylsiloxane (PDMS), poly(acrylic acid), poly(vinyl alcohol), polyamide, polyarylamide, poly(acrylamide), poly(acrylic anhydride), poly(vinyl naphthalene), poly(styrene), cellulose acetate, polyacrylonitrile, polysulfone, poly(vinylidene fluoride), poly(ethylene terephthalate), poly(ethylene-co-vinyl acetate), poly(vinyl chloride). 9. The composite elastomer of Claim 1, wherein the platelet-like fillers comprise graphene oxide (GO). 10. The composite elastomer of Claim 1, wherein the platelet-like fillers have an average aspect ratio between 100 and 2000. 11. The composite elastomer of Claim 1 , wherein the total weight of the plurality of polymers is at least 70% by weight of the total weight of only the plurality of polymers and the plurality of platelet-like fillers. 12. The composite elastomer of Claim 1, wherein at least 75% of the plurality of polymers and plurality of platelet-like fillers, by weight, are crosslinked. 13. The composite elastomer of Claim 1, wherein the plurality of plateletlike fillers are aligned approximately parallel to a bottom surface of the composite elastomer. 14. The composite elastomer of Claim 1, wherein the plurality of plateletlike fillers are homogenously distributed in the composite elastomer. 15. The composite elastomer of Claim 1, wherein the composite elastomer has sufficient tensile strength to be stretched up to 100% of its length before failure. 16. The composite elastomer of Claim 1, wherein the composite elastomer is in the form of a membrane has a gas permeability for a single gas that is reduced by at least 45% as compared to a similar material comprising only the plurality of polymers and not the plurality of platelet-like fillers. 17. The composite elastomer of Claim 1, wherein the composite elastomer is in the form of a membrane and has a gas selectivity of at least 10 for C0 2 in a gas mixture. 18. A method of forming a composite elastomer, the method comprising reacting amine or amide functional groups, or a combination thereof, on a plurality of polymers with epoxide functional groups on a plurality of platelet-like fillers to form an ethanolamine or ethanolamide bond and thereby crosslink the plurality of polymers via the plurality of platelet-like fillers. 19. The method of Claim 18, wherein the plurality of polymers are flexible at 21 Â°C. 20. The method of Claim 18, wherein the amine or amide functional groups comprise primary amines, secondary amines, secondary amides, and combinations thereof. 21. The method of Claim 18, wherein the amine or amide functional groups comprise between 0.05% by weight and 4.00% by weight of the plurality of polymers. 22. The method of Claim 18, wherein the plurality of polymers are telechelic for the amine or amide functional groups, of a combination thereof. 23. The method of Claim 22, wherein the amine or amide functional groups on both ends of the polymers react with the epoxide functional groups on the plurality of platelet-like fillers. 24. The method of Claim 18, further comprising reacting a plurality of additional polymers with the plurality of polymers to form covalent bonds there between, wherein the plurality of additional polymers do not react with the plurality of platelet-like fillers. 25. The method of Claim 18, further comprising forming a solution of the plurality of polymers and plurality of platelet-like fillers in a solvent. 26. The method of Claim 25, wherein the solution comprises at least 1% plurality of polymers by weight of the total weight of plurality of polymers and solvent. 27. The method of Claim 25, wherein the solution has a viscosity of at least 1 cSt. 28. The method of Claim 25, further comprising solution casting the solution to form the composite elastomer. 29. The method of Claim 25, further comprising spin-coating the solution to form the composite elastomer. 30. A gas separation membrane comprising the composite elastomer of any of Claims 1-17 or a composite elastomer formed using a method of Claims 18-29. 31. A gas separation system comprising a gas separation membrane comprising the composite elastomer of any of Claims 1-17 or a composite elastomer formed using a method of Claims 18-29. 32. A gas barrier membrane comprising the composite elastomer of any of Claims 1-17 or a composite elastomer formed using a method of Claims 18-29.
